mission.title: Install and Configure Cluster API (CAPI)
mission.description: Cluster API (CAPI) is a Kubernetes operator that provides declarative APIs and tooling to simplify the provisioning, upgrading, and management of multiple Kubernetes clusters. It is particularly useful for platform operators who need to manage clusters across various infrastructure environments consistently. Key trade-offs include the complexity of setup and the need for familiarity with Kubernetes concepts.

steps.title: Add the Cluster API Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official Cluster API Helm repository to your local Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add cluster-api https://charts.cluster-api.sigs.k8s.io

```

> This repository contains the necessary charts for installing Cluster API.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are required for Cluster API to function correctly, as they define the custom resources used by the operator.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command installs the CRDs for Cluster API version 1.12.4.

steps.title: Install the Cluster API controller
steps.description:
This step installs the Cluster API controller using Helm, which manages the lifecycle of clusters.

```bash

helm install cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

```

> Ensure you have the correct namespace and version specified.

steps.title: Create a Cluster API custom resource
steps.description:
This step creates a custom resource that defines a new Kubernetes cluster managed by Cluster API.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

```

> Replace 'MyInfrastructureProviderCluster' with your specific infrastructure provider.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Cluster API installation
steps.description:
This step checks if the Cluster API components are running correctly.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

```

> You should see the Cluster API controller pods running.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

uninstall.title: Uninstall Cluster API
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Cluster API installation and all associated resources. Be aware that this may lead to data loss for any clusters managed by Cluster API.

```bash

helm uninstall cluster-api --namespace kube-system

kubectl delete -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

```

> This command removes the Cluster API controller.
> This command removes the CRDs associated with Cluster API.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Cluster API
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your cluster configurations. This step will upgrade the Cluster API controller and verify the installation afterward.

```bash

kubectl get clusters -o yaml > cluster-backup.yaml

helm upgrade cluster-api cluster-api/cluster-api --namespace kube-system --version 1.12.4

kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

```

> Backup your current cluster configurations.
> This command upgrades the Cluster API controller.
> Verify that the Cluster API controller pods are running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster CRD is not installed or not available in the cluster.

**Fix:**
Reapply the CRD using the command: kubectl apply -f https://github.com/kubernetes-sigs/cluster-api/releases/download/v1.12.4/cluster-api.crds.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'failed to connect to the API server'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This may occur if the Kubernetes API server is down or if there are network issues.

**Fix:**
Check the status of the Kubernetes API server and ensure network connectivity.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'unable to recognize "": no matches for kind "Cluster" in version "cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1"'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API controller is not running or is misconfigured.

**Fix:**
Check the logs of the Cluster API controller pod using: kubectl logs -n kube-system <pod-name>

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'no resources found in kube-system namespace'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the Cluster API components have not been installed correctly.

**Fix:**
Reinstall the Cluster API components following the installation steps.

versionNotes.changes: Improved support for multi-cluster management, added new infrastructure providers.
versionNotes.deprecations: Deprecated support for older Kubernetes versions below 1.26.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v1.11.x, ensure to update your CRDs and check for any breaking changes in the new version.

resolution.summary: You should see the Cluster API controller pods running in the kube-system namespace with a status of 'Running'. Use the command: kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
kind: Cluster
metadata:
  name: my-cluster
  namespace: default
spec:
  infrastructureRef:
    apiVersion: infrastructure.cluster.x-k8s.io/v1beta1
    kind: MyInfrastructureProviderCluster
    name: my-infrastructure
EOF
kubectl get pods -n kube-system | grep cluster-api

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster running version 1.26 or higher and have kubectl and Helm installed.
